isolation.surface: rgoc CLI + generated Go artifacts + local HTTP probe

assertions.title: Named rgo functions can be passed directly as handlers

steps.action: Compile the named-handler reuse fixture through rgoc
steps.expected: rgoc emits Go successfully
steps.observed: rgoc exited 0 and wrote /home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/evidence/callback-slice/callback-slice-cli/generated/named-handler-reuse-server.go

steps.action: Validate generated Go with gofmt -d, go build, and go vet
steps.expected: All downstream Go checks succeed
steps.observed: gofmt=0, go build=0, go vet=0

steps.action: Start the evidence-local binary and request GET /items
steps.expected: Named handler returns 200 with its response body
steps.observed:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 17:16:43 GMT
Content-Length: 22
Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8

named handler response

assertions.title: Zero-capture anonymous handlers lower to Go func literals

steps.action: Compile the mixed-handler fixture with an inline zero-capture lambda
steps.expected: rgoc emits Go containing a func literal at HandleFunc
steps.observed: Generated Go contains mux.HandleFunc("/anon", func(...))

steps.action: Validate generated Go with gofmt -d, go build, and go vet
steps.expected: All downstream Go checks succeed
steps.observed: gofmt=0, go build=0, go vet=0

steps.action: Start the server and request GET /anon
steps.expected: Inline anonymous handler returns 200 with its body
steps.observed:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 17:16:44 GMT
Content-Length: 26
Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8

anonymous handler response

assertions.title: Capturing anonymous handlers are rejected in this mission

steps.action: Compile a fixture whose anonymous handler captures greeting
steps.expected: rgoc rejects the capture before Go generation
steps.observed:
warning: Git tree '/home/lenstr/workspace/personal/hack/rgo-lang' is dirty
error: /home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/evidence/callback-slice/callback-slice-cli/fixtures/capturing-anonymous-handler.rg:7:17: undefined name 'greeting'

steps.action: Check requested output path
steps.expected: No Go output file exists
steps.observed: output exists=False

assertions.title: Callback signature mismatches fail in rgoc

steps.action: Compile wrong-arity handler fixture
steps.expected: rgoc rejects the callback signature
steps.observed:
warning: Git tree '/home/lenstr/workspace/personal/hack/rgo-lang' is dirty
error: /home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/evidence/callback-slice/callback-slice-cli/fixtures/wrong-arity-handler.rg:7:9: type mismatch: expected fn(http::ResponseWriter, http::Request) -> (), got fn(http::ResponseWriter) -> ()

steps.action: Compile wrong-parameter-type handler fixture
steps.expected: rgoc rejects the callback signature
steps.observed:
warning: Git tree '/home/lenstr/workspace/personal/hack/rgo-lang' is dirty
error: /home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/evidence/callback-slice/callback-slice-cli/fixtures/wrong-param-type-handler.rg:7:9: type mismatch: expected fn(http::ResponseWriter, http::Request) -> (), got fn(i64, i64) -> ()

steps.action: Compile wrong-return-type handler fixture
steps.expected: rgoc rejects the callback signature
steps.observed:
warning: Git tree '/home/lenstr/workspace/personal/hack/rgo-lang' is dirty
error: /home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/evidence/callback-slice/callback-slice-cli/fixtures/wrong-return-type-handler.rg:7:9: type mismatch: expected fn(http::ResponseWriter, http::Request) -> (), got fn(http::ResponseWriter, http::Request) -> i64

steps.action: Check requested output paths
steps.expected: No Go output files exist for any rejected fixture
steps.observed: wrong_arity=False, wrong_param=False, wrong_return=False

assertions.title: Non-callable values are rejected at handler registration

steps.action: Compile fixture passing a literal integer to handle_func
steps.expected: rgoc rejects the non-callable expression
steps.observed:
warning: Git tree '/home/lenstr/workspace/personal/hack/rgo-lang' is dirty
error: /home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/evidence/callback-slice/callback-slice-cli/fixtures/non-callable-literal-handler.rg:4:9: not callable: expected fn(http::ResponseWriter, http::Request) -> (), but got non-callable type i64

steps.action: Compile fixture passing a bound integer variable to handle_func
steps.expected: rgoc rejects the non-callable bound value
steps.observed:
warning: Git tree '/home/lenstr/workspace/personal/hack/rgo-lang' is dirty
error: /home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/evidence/callback-slice/callback-slice-cli/fixtures/non-callable-bound-handler.rg:5:9: not callable: expected fn(http::ResponseWriter, http::Request) -> (), but got non-callable type i64

steps.action: Check requested output paths
steps.expected: No Go output files exist for either rejected fixture
steps.observed: literal=False, bound=False

assertions.title: Handler registration does not consume named functions or zero-capture anonymous handlers

steps.action: Compile a fixture that registers the same named handler on /items and /other
steps.expected: Both registrations compile without consuming the handler
steps.observed: Generated Go contains both HandleFunc calls using the same handler symbol

steps.action: Validate generated Go with gofmt -d, go build, and go vet
steps.expected: All downstream Go checks succeed
steps.observed: gofmt=0, go build=0, go vet=0

steps.action: Run the server and request both registered routes
steps.expected: Both routes remain callable and return the handler response
steps.observed:
/items => HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 17:16:43 GMT
Content-Length: 22
Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8

named handler response ; /other => HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 17:16:43 GMT
Content-Length: 22
Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8

named handler response

evidence.network: GET /items -> 200; GET /other -> 200 using the same named handler binding

assertions.title: Registered handlers remain callable across multiple requests without ownership regressions

steps.action: Start the named-handler reuse server once
steps.expected: One process remains available for repeated probes
steps.observed: Server binary started and served repeated requests on 127.0.0.1:3111

steps.action: Request GET /items twice against the same process
steps.expected: Both requests return 200 with the same body
steps.observed:
first=HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 17:16:43 GMT
Content-Length: 22
Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8

named handler response ; second=HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 17:16:43 GMT
Content-Length: 22
Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8

named handler response

steps.action: Request GET /other on the same process after the repeated /items calls
steps.expected: Other route still succeeds
steps.observed:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 17:16:43 GMT
Content-Length: 22
Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8

named handler response

evidence.network: GET /items -> 200 twice; GET /other -> 200 on the same running process

assertions.title: A mixed named-handler and zero-capture-lambda server works end-to-end

steps.action: Compile the mixed named-handler + anonymous-handler fixture
steps.expected: rgoc emits valid Go for both callback forms
steps.observed: Generated Go contains both mux.HandleFunc("/named", named_handler) and mux.HandleFunc("/anon", func(...))

steps.action: Validate generated Go with gofmt -d, go build, and go vet
steps.expected: All downstream Go checks succeed
steps.observed: gofmt=0, go build=0, go vet=0

steps.action: Run the server and request GET /named and GET /anon
steps.expected: Both routes return their distinct success bodies
steps.observed:
/named => HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 17:16:44 GMT
Content-Length: 22
Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8

named handler response ; /anon => HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 17:16:44 GMT
Content-Length: 26
Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8

anonymous handler response

evidence.network: GET /named -> 200 named handler response; GET /anon -> 200 anonymous handler response

frictions.description: Each `nix develop -c ...` invocation prints the flake dev-shell banner to stdout, so clean `gofmt -d` evidence logs are not empty even when no diff is emitted.
frictions.resolution: Validated formatter stability by checking the `gofmt -d` exit code and confirming the stdout logs contained only the banner text, not a diff.

summary: Tested 8 assertions: 8 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked, 0 skipped.
